risk: Audit status can drift from the merged repo truth.
mitigation: Cross-check audit ledger status against this TSV source.
closure: Closed and verified by known gaps freshness validation.
notes: Audit status drift is blocked.

risk: Plans can pass structure while using weak sources.
mitigation: Require concrete source checks that match changed targets or canonical files.
closure: Closed by stricter source checks.
notes: Route source checks are enforced.

risk: Connector evidence can be listed without target impact.
mitigation: Require source action result decision and target linkage.
closure: Closed and verified by connector target-link simulations.
notes: Structural connector impact is blocked.

risk: Progress notes can be backfilled after work ends.
mitigation: Require ordered start mid and pre-merge checkpoints.
closure: Closed and verified by ordered lifecycle fixtures.
notes: Structural backfill is blocked.

risk: Lessons can have headings but weak content.
mitigation: Require concrete cause evidence regression and prevention content.
closure: Closed and verified by shallow lesson negative fixtures.
notes: Structural weak lesson closure is blocked.

risk: Reusable assets can be used without feedback or with feedback for the wrong asset.
mitigation: Require exact declared asset coverage, confidence evidence, rating outcome and decision evidence, or waiver.
closure: Closed by exact reusable asset feedback evidence checks and negative fixtures.
notes: Score truthfulness remains review-based, but missing, partial, or unrelated feedback is blocked.

risk: Project planning documentation assets can be skipped even when they contain required architecture domain integration deployment or product guidance.
mitigation: Require Route Plan evidence for relevant documentation assets or a concrete documentation asset selection waiver.
closure: Closed by deterministic documentation/reference asset evidence or waiver enforcement for code/config/test changes.
notes: Covers documentation assets that explain how to plan and build code projects.

risk: RTK can be mentioned without auditable decision impact.
mitigation: Require source action result decision prior assumption finding impact target confidence and limitation evidence, or explicit waiver.
closure: Closed by RTK impact evidence checks with target linkage and negative fixtures.
notes: Unobservable reasoning cannot be proven, but generic RTK mentions and missing impact evidence are blocked.

risk: Graph output can exist without target use.
mitigation: Require structured graph usage evidence linked to the write target.
closure: Closed and verified by loose and wrong-target negative fixtures.
notes: Structural graph use is now target linked.

risk: Hygiene issues can hide beyond simple checks.
mitigation: Add CI policy gates for repeated symbols and stale references.
closure: Closed by CI policy gates.
notes: Structural hygiene cases are blocked.

risk: Review evidence can be absent or vague.
mitigation: Require PR body review evidence with structured fields.
closure: Closed and verified by PR policy evidence gate.
notes: Structural review evidence is blocked.

risk: Repair path lacks observed negative run evidence.
mitigation: Simulate safe negative main validation issue creation path.
closure: Closed by deterministic fake-gh repair issue simulation.
notes: Safe simulation now observes the repair issue path without failing main.

risk: Connector selection rules cover only nine connectors, so newer connector-backed work can skip required sources silently.
mitigation: Move selection rules into a data-driven manifest tied to the external-systems inventory with coverage tests.
closure: Closed by manifest-driven selection covering the connector inventory, verified by coverage and precision fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR B; right-connector judgment stays review-based by design.

risk: Connector result evidence accepts free prose, so vague or unrelated results can pass the usage gate.
mitigation: Require each result line to carry a concrete identifier such as a path, issue or PR number, or SHA.
closure: Closed by requiring Connector Usage Evidence result lines to include concrete identifiers with vague-result and PR/path positive fixtures.
notes: Deep semantic use remains review-based by design, but identifier-free result prose is blocked.

risk: Required-template detection maps eleven template ids while the templates inventory holds about twenty-six.
mitigation: Move template keyword rules into a manifest and add a coverage test tying every template directory to a rule or exemption.
closure: Closed by manifest rules or exemptions for every template directory, verified by coverage and precision fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR B; over-matching guarded by precision fixtures.

risk: Pattern gating checks read evidence only after a plan declares patterns, so required patterns can be omitted from plans.
mitigation: Add a registry-driven required-pattern checker keyed on task class, domain tags, and target paths with a waiver path.
closure: Closed by the registry-driven required-pattern gate in the write gate, verified by domain, waiver, and fail-closed fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR B; pattern-fit judgment stays review-based by design.

risk: Skill selection rules cover five skills, so newer skills and task classes can be skipped without a waiver.
mitigation: Extend the required-skills rules and add a coverage test tying the skills inventory to a rule or explicit default note.
closure: Closed by inventory-tied skill rules, verified by missing-skill, waiver, precision, and coverage fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR B; runtime skill evidence stays enforced.

risk: Changed files never imply capabilities, so a plan can omit a capability its staged changes clearly exercise.
mitigation: Add a staged-change guard mapping changed paths to implied capability ids that must appear in plan evidence or waiver.
closure: Closed by the staged-change guard in capability-evidence-policy CI, verified by declared, missing, waived, and stale-map fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR B; stale declared capabilities accepted by design to avoid false blocks.

risk: Significant agent runs outside the current path list can skip the run trace, and waiver bodies are not validated.
mitigation: Extend deterministic trace triggers to patterns, templates, commands, and large ranges, and validate waiver bodies.
closure: Closed by extended deterministic triggers and waiver-body validation, verified by trigger and heading-only-waiver fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR C; tightening only, existing waiver fixture unchanged.

risk: Two waived simulation cells are directly fixture-testable, and by-design cells are not machine-distinguishable from lazy waivers.
mitigation: Replace replaceable waivers with real fixtures and add an explicit non-waivable token to the coverage schema.
closure: Closed by the none-by-design token with design-wording rejection and by replacing both replaceable cells with real fixtures, verified by token and fixture tests.
notes: Closed in readiness PR C; stale-deferred blocking behavior preserved.

risk: A declared stack with a missing lint or test tool warns instead of failing in every environment including CI.
mitigation: Hard-fail missing tools for declared stacks in CI and require a named local waiver variable with a reason.
closure: Closed by the CI hard-fail and named local waiver contract, verified by CI, no-waiver, named-waiver, and wrong-tool fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR C; repos with no declared stack stay unaffected.

risk: Three gates select the newest plan by file time, so a wrong plan can satisfy gates in multi-plan sessions.
mitigation: Add shared target-aware plan selection that fails closed when multiple recent plans conflict for a write target.
closure: Closed by shared target-aware selection in all three plan-consuming gates, verified by wrong-newest, override, and fallback fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR C; EOS_ACTIVE_PLAN and active.md precedence preserved.

risk: The PR review evidence validator is inline workflow python, so shallow or vague review evidence cases are not fixture-tested.
mitigation: Extract the validator to a script, require checks to name real gates, and add shallow-review negative fixtures.
closure: Closed in readiness PR D; extracted validator requires checks: to name a real gate and evidence: to be a concrete reference, with shallow/vague negative fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR D; deep review quality stays review-based by design, but shallow/vague evidence is blocked.

risk: No deterministic merge-readiness artifact is required on PRs, so base, head SHA, and approval evidence stay unstructured.
mitigation: Require a Merge Readiness PR-body section with base, expected head SHA, CI, threads, and approval fields validated in CI.
closure: Closed in readiness PR D; the PR-body artifact is validated with missing, placeholder, and SHA-mismatch fixtures.
notes: Closed in readiness PR D; the merge decision itself stays human by design, but the evidence artifact is enforced.

risk: The install contract validates output shape, so downstream runtime behavior in a generated target project is untested.
mitigation: Extend the generated-target simulation to exercise tool fallbacks, commands, learning loop, and gate behavior after install.
closure: Closed in readiness PR E; downstream behavior tests cover tool-absence fallback, command parsing, the learning-loop fix-needs-test gate, templates/patterns reachability, and the tool-environment contract in a generated target, plus a manifest-driven install-copy fix and coverage fixture for the script-dependency bug PR D's review found.
notes: Closed in readiness PR E; contract-shape and downstream runtime behavior are both enforced.

risk: Without a deterministic per-PR check, a PR could silently omit or misdeclare which result-loop contract governs a code/config/test change, letting reviewers and future scaling audits assume coverage that a diff never actually selected or validated.
mitigation: Manifest-completeness dimension stays closed via the dedicated named enforcement-tests.yml step (check-result-loop-contract.py, PR #228). Per-PR declaration dimension: scripts/monitoring/collect-pr-work-history.py derives selected_result_loop_contract deterministically from changed paths (templates/<alias>/... maps to the project type that owns that alias per project-type-roadmaps.tsv, so templates/rag-system correctly resolves to ai-agent; known Engineering OS governance/tooling paths map to a status=exempt sentinel, engineering-os-governance, registered in result-loop-requirements.tsv and project-type-roadmaps.tsv so it does not pull in check-scaling-extension.py's active-project coverage requirements; any other path, such as a downstream installed target project's own application source, is left unclassified rather than guessed), falling back to a minimal declared selected_result_loop_contract: PR-body field only when derivation is genuinely ambiguous or unclassified, validated against both the real manifest id set and the actual diff candidates. scripts/enforcement/check-operational-work-history-evidence.sh validates the artifact's result_loop_contract object directly, never re-parsing the PR body, and fails closed on missing/unknown-id/placeholder/ambiguous/declared-unrelated-to-diff cases. check-route-plan-contract.sh remains unwired by design; its 8-field requirement was not revived.
closure: Closed and verified: PR #239 (github.com/yotamfried-ux/Engineering-OS/pull/239) is real, non-fixture positive evidence — CI green and the generated artifact derived selected_result_loop_contract=engineering-os-governance from a governance-only diff. PR #240 (github.com/yotamfried-ux/Engineering-OS/pull/240) is real negative evidence — a genuinely ambiguous diff reproducibly failed real pr-policy with the expected ambiguous/undeclared candidates and was closed without merging. The real review findings on PR #239 were fixed and resolved; check-route-plan-contract.sh stays unwired.
notes: Implemented and fixture-tested the per-PR declaration dimension and closed it with real positive PR #239 and real negative PR #240 evidence. monitoring-metrics-sufficiency and project-8-real-run-evidence remain separate open gaps.

risk: check-scaling-extension.py now runs as a dedicated named CI step on every real PR, confirmed green against real PR #229 content and blocking incomplete project-type extensions.
mitigation: Wired through enforcement-tests.yml using the same named-step pattern as readiness and result-loop checks.
closure: Closed and verified by the named real-PR CI step plus fixtures.
notes: Closed 2026-07-08 in PR #229 after confirming the gate runs against real PR content and no scaling-specific per-PR declaration blind spot remained.

risk: Claude runs can consume usage and produce governance changes without recording whether Engineering OS improved or harmed behavior, efficiency, evidence quality, friction, false positives, and decision quality.
mitigation: Require structured Operational Behavior Evidence for significant PRs, including behavior summary, Engineering OS influence, efficiency signals, friction or false positives, quality signals, usage surrogate, and next system improvement.
closure: Closed by wiring check-operational-behavior-evidence.sh into check-pr-review-evidence.sh, invoked by pr-policy on every PR, with missing, partial, and complete fixtures.
notes: This is intentionally broader than usage-only tracking; it measures how Engineering OS affects work quality and efficiency.

risk: 10 kind=project templates lacked roadmap entries, so the Scaling Gate never covered them.
mitigation: Added and activated roadmap plus result-loop, documentation, pattern, and skill coverage for every uncovered project template, with a completeness rule.
closure: Closed: all 10 project types carry real active coverage across all 5 required manifests, landed across PR #230 and the Automation/Data backfill PR.
notes: Closed 2026-07-08 after both backfill PRs landed and real CI validated all five manifests.

risk: Exporter, importer, analyzer and privacy tests exist, but no valid target-project session telemetry run has been imported. Project 8 PR #4 produced real OWH-only evidence with `telemetry_available=false` and zero events, so monitoring sufficiency still cannot be judged.
mitigation: Keep OWH, session telemetry and product outcomes separate. Run a fresh Project 8 session only after exact-workspace installation and positive preflight, then export, import and analyze a non-empty bundle.
closure: Open until at least one valid target-project telemetry run is imported and analyzed and the audit monitoring row is updated from real findings.
notes: Depends on the open project-8-real-run-evidence gap; PR #244 removed the installation/session blocker but did not create telemetry evidence for the completed run.

risk: Project 8 PR #4 is a real Engineering OS target run with valid Operational Work History and concrete product improvements, but its target workspace lacked `.claude/settings.json`; the artifact records `telemetry_available=false`, zero events and 25 repeated-cycle commits. No non-empty telemetry bundle has been exported, imported or analyzed.
mitigation: Install current Engineering OS in the exact Project 8 Claude workspace, open a new session, require positive `require-telemetry-session.sh` preflight, perform a bounded Project 8 task, export/import/analyze the non-empty run and review findings.
closure: Open until a fresh instrumented Project 8 run satisfies preflight, export, import, analysis and reviewed-findings requirements. OWH alone cannot close it.
notes: Changed from blocked to open because Engineering OS PR #244 removed the canonical installer/session/preflight blocker. The closure bar remains unchanged and is not satisfied.

risk: Real-PR evidence exists across PRs #234, #235 and the blocked case #236, proving the CI-generated artifact and gate against real data.
mitigation: Require the CI-generated `.engineering-os/work-history/latest.json`, real GitHub/CI metadata and existing learning-loop routing for PRs with changed files.
closure: Closed 2026-07-09 after the real positive and negative evidence sequence satisfied the rollout closure bar.
notes: The foundation is closed, while monitoring-metrics-sufficiency and project-8-real-run-evidence remain separate open gaps.

risk: Claude Code merges hooks across settings scopes rather than overriding (confirmed against official docs). Without a guard, a repo that already has its own working project-local .claude/settings.json would fire its own hooks directly AND the new user-level dispatcher (added for multi-repo Remote sessions) would also resolve to the same repo, recording every event twice and corrupting event counts for that repo's telemetry bundle.
mitigation: Added has_conflicting_project_local_hooks() in telemetry_repo_discovery.py: the dispatcher never records for a repo whose own .claude/settings.json already carries Engineering-OS-owned hook markers. Traded a small completeness loss (such a repo gets zero dispatcher-side telemetry if the session did not actually start inside it) for correctness (never double-count). Documented as a deliberate, conservative choice, not silently unhandled.
closure: Mitigated in simulation: a fixture with both a self-sufficient repo and a dispatcher-only sibling proves the self-sufficient repo receives zero dispatcher-recorded events while the sibling still gets recorded normally. Not yet proven against a real Claude Code Remote host session — see the real-Remote-experiment requirement in .claude/plans/remote-multirepo-telemetry-hooks.md.
notes: Found during user review of the multi-repo telemetry dispatch feature, before merge. Not closed: closure requires the real Remote experiment, same bar as multirepo-remote-telemetry-validation below.

risk: The new multi-repo telemetry dispatch (user-level $HOME/.claude/settings.json bootstrap, repository discovery, per-event attribution) is implemented and covered by simulated fixture tests only. No real Claude Code Remote session — one that genuinely starts outside any managed repository, with the user-level hooks actually installed by the real Claude Code runtime, not by a test harness — has exercised it yet. The official hooks docs do not explicitly guarantee that a hook subprocess's own OS-level cwd equals the JSON payload's cwd field, which the dispatcher depends on for attribution; this is an unverified assumption until a real run proves it.
mitigation: Do not claim this feature works in Claude Code Remote until a real session run confirms: SessionStart fires from the user-level install, only genuinely managed sibling repos are discovered, an unmanaged sibling collects nothing, a real tool event in a managed repo lands in that repo's own bundle, and PR-matching selects the correct PR from a non-empty bundle. A short runbook for this experiment is included in the Route Plan.
closure: Open until a real Claude Code Remote experiment (not simulation) produces the evidence listed in the mitigation column and is recorded in the PR. Related to, but distinct from, the already-open monitoring-metrics-sufficiency and project-8-real-run-evidence gaps — neither of those two rows is being closed or reinterpreted by this one; this row covers the dispatch mechanism itself, those two cover Project 8's own end-to-end telemetry evidence.
notes: Added alongside the dispatch feature itself so the real-Remote-experiment requirement is tracked as a first-class, separately-closeable gap rather than only living in a PR checklist that could be forgotten after merge.

risk: This repository's own installed .claude/settings.json is stale relative to the current patch-settings-telemetry.py: Stop/StopFailure/SessionEnd hooks call scripts/monitoring/eos-telemetry-event.sh directly instead of scripts/monitoring/record-and-sync-telemetry.sh (which additionally pushes the durable remote handoff/sync on those boundary events). Confirmed non-blocking today only because this repository's own .engineering-os/telemetry-policy.json is absent, so load_policy() resolves mode=disabled and sync()/check_state() both no-op regardless of which script path is called (verified directly against telemetry_handoff.load_policy this session, not assumed). It would become a real, silent gap the moment this repository's own telemetry policy is ever set to required or best_effort.
mitigation: Re-running scripts/monitoring/install-policy-gates.sh (or the equivalent direct-mode patch-settings-telemetry.py invocation) against this repo's own .claude/settings.json would resolve the drift; deliberately not done as part of the multirepo-dispatch feature to keep that change minimal and scoped, per this repo's own don't-touch-downstream-unnecessarily principle. NOT a blocker for this feature's own PR: confirmed does not affect scenario A (existing single-repo regression baseline) or scenario G (downstream compatibility) since it predates this feature and this repository is not itself in required/best_effort mode.
closure: Open until either this repository's own .claude/settings.json is re-synced with the current patcher, or this repository adopts a required/best_effort telemetry policy (at which point re-syncing becomes mandatory, not optional).
notes: Found incidentally while testing the user-level installer against this repository's real settings.json this session. Explicitly not fixed here — separate, pre-existing repo-hygiene issue, unrelated to the multirepo-dispatch architecture itself.
